# Wyoming’s Fast Start Holds Up in 45-38 Win Over Akron

Akron, OH — Wyoming didn’t wait to establish control. **Eric Richardson’s 8:49 first-quarter kickoff return for a touchdown** set the tone, and the Cowboys never trailed, building a multi-score cushion that proved just enough in a 45-38 victory over Akron to open the 2025 season.

Wyoming moves to 1-0 with the win, while Akron drops to 0-1. In a matchup between two evenly matched programs, the Cowboys’ early surge and efficient passing from quarterback Kaden Anderson created separation that withstood a late Akron push.

## Early cushion proves decisive

The game’s rhythm was set in a frantic first quarter. After Richardson’s return made it 7-0, Akron answered twice to tie the game, but Wyoming responded each time. **Terron Kellman’s rushing touchdown and a 1:01 scoring strike from Anderson to Michael Fitzgerald** pushed the Cowboys ahead 21-14 by the end of the opening period.

Wyoming extended the margin in the second quarter. Erik Sandvik added a field goal, and after Akron trimmed the deficit to seven, **Anderson connected with John Michael Gyllenborg with 0:37 left before halftime**, sending Wyoming into the break with a 31-17 lead.

That advantage grew in the third quarter. **Fitzgerald’s second touchdown reception and Kellman’s second rushing score** stretched the lead to 45-24, marking Akron’s largest deficit at 21 points.

## Anderson’s efficiency, Kellman’s burst lead offense

Wyoming’s offense was defined by precision and balance, even in a game with limited possession time.

**Anderson completed 24 of 28 passes for 282 yards and three touchdowns**, consistently finding open targets and finishing drives. Fitzgerald was his primary red-zone option, catching six passes for 34 yards and two scores, while Jaylen Sargent added five receptions for 67 yards.

Kellman delivered the explosive plays on the ground, **rushing 12 times for 154 yards and two touchdowns** and adding five catches for 40 yards. His scoring runs in the first and third quarters helped Wyoming maintain control whenever Akron threatened.

## Akron’s passing surge tightens the finish

Akron’s offense, led by quarterback Ben Finley, kept the pressure on into the fourth quarter. **Finley threw for 392 yards and four touchdowns**, spreading the ball across multiple targets.

Jordan Gant was a central figure, contributing **66 rushing yards and a touchdown on the ground while adding seven receptions for 136 yards and two receiving scores**. Marcel Williams (nine catches, 94 yards, touchdown) and Israel Polk (seven catches, 75 yards, touchdown) also played key roles.

The Zips closed the gap late with two fourth-quarter touchdown passes from Finley — one to Williams and another to Gant — cutting the score to 45-38 with 2:14 remaining. Wyoming, however, held on from there to secure the win.

## Defense bends but holds late

Wyoming’s defense faced a high-volume passing attack, with Akron totaling 502 yards and 24 first downs. Evan Eller led the Cowboys with nine tackles, while Dainsus Miller and Jones Thomas added seven each.

Despite allowing 21 second-half points, Wyoming’s earlier stops and the offense’s 45-point output ensured Akron never erased the deficit entirely.

## A strong opening statement

Wyoming finished with 648 total yards and scored in every quarter it needed to, building a lead that defined the game from the outset. The Cowboys’ combination of early explosiveness and sustained offensive execution delivered a season-opening win — even as Akron’s late rally made the margin anything but comfortable.

Laramie, WY — Wyoming erased a 14-point deficit and took control in the second half, with **Sam Scott’s 1:56 rushing touchdown in the fourth quarter pushing the Cowboys ahead 30-17**, a margin that held in a 30-24 win over Utah.

That lead expanded in the fourth quarter. Scott scored from the ground at 8:56 to make it 23-14, and after Utah trimmed the margin to six, **Scott’s second rushing touchdown with 1:56 remaining stretched the lead back to 13 points at 30-17**.

Quarterback Kaden Anderson continued his strong early-season play, **completing 24 of 33 passes for 300 yards and two touchdowns without an interception**. His efficiency helped Wyoming convert 7 of 10 third downs and maintain control of possession for over 20 minutes.

Wyoming answered quickly. Fitzgerald’s touchdown reception cut the deficit in half, and **Gyllenborg’s touchdown catch with 0:24 remaining before halftime tied the game at 14**, completing the turnaround before the break.

From there, Wyoming never relinquished the lead.

## Statement result for Wyoming

Against a heavily favored opponent, Wyoming produced its most complete performance of the season, overcoming an early deficit with efficiency on offense and timely defense.

The Cowboys finished with 432 total yards, no turnovers, and a 7-of-10 mark on third down — a profile that supported a comeback and ultimately secured a 30-24 win.

# Wyoming Stays Unbeaten, Powers Past Colorado 38-28 Behind Ground Game, Defensive Score

Laramie, WY — **Caleb Robinson’s 4:17 fourth-quarter fumble return for a touchdown** created separation, and Wyoming followed with another scoring drive to secure a 38-28 win over Colorado, pushing the Cowboys to 4-0 on the season.

Wyoming, which entered on a three-game winning streak, extended it to four with another result against a heavily favored opponent. Colorado falls to 2-2. The Cowboys never trailed, building leads in each half and answering every push from the Buffaloes.

## Defensive touchdown swings fourth quarter

The game tightened midway through the fourth quarter when Colorado cut the deficit to 23-21 on a touchdown pass from Kaidon Salter to Dre’lon Miller with 6:33 remaining.

Wyoming responded immediately on defense. **Robinson’s fumble return touchdown at 4:17 pushed the lead to 30-21**, restoring a two-possession margin. Colorado answered again with a touchdown pass to Sincere Brown at 2:46, trimming the score to 30-28.

The Cowboys delivered the final blow on the ground. **Terron Kellman’s 2:11 rushing touchdown extended the lead to 37-28**, sealing the outcome in a game where Wyoming controlled the lead throughout.

## Ground attack leads the way

Wyoming’s offense leaned heavily on its rushing production, finishing with 193 yards on 23 carries.

Kellman led the effort with **18 carries for 175 yards and a touchdown**, consistently generating chunk plays. Senior running back Damashja Harris added two short touchdown runs on just two carries, both coming in key moments — one late in the second quarter and another early in the third.

Quarterback Kaden Anderson contributed a rushing touchdown as well, opening the scoring in the first quarter. Through the air, Anderson **completed 22 of 36 passes for 251 yards**, spreading the ball to multiple targets despite an interception.

Jaylen Sargent led Wyoming receivers with six catches for 57 yards, while tight end John Michael Gyllenborg added five receptions for 65 yards.

## Colorado leans on Salter, Brown

Colorado’s offense generated 433 total yards, driven primarily by its passing game.

Salter **completed 25 of 32 passes for 381 yards and two touchdowns**, while also rushing for two scores. Brown was his top target, finishing with **11 catches for 198 yards and a touchdown**, accounting for a significant portion of the Buffaloes’ offense.

Despite the production, Colorado struggled to establish a running game, finishing with 21 yards on 14 carries.

## Wyoming builds control early

Wyoming set the tone in the first half. Anderson’s rushing touchdown gave the Cowboys a 7-0 lead late in the first quarter, and Erik Sandvik’s field goal extended it to 10-0 early in the second.

Colorado answered with a pair of rushing touchdowns from Salter, but Wyoming maintained the edge. **Harris’ touchdown run at 1:35 of the second quarter kept the Cowboys in front**, and they carried a 16-14 lead into halftime.

The Cowboys extended that margin early in the third quarter when Harris scored again, pushing the lead to 23-14.

## Unbeaten through four

Wyoming finished with 554 total yards, converted 7 of 12 third downs, and forced two turnovers, including the decisive fumble return.

Through four games, the Cowboys have combined offensive production with timely defense, adding another win against a Big 12 opponent and maintaining an undefeated start to the 2025 season.

# Wyoming Rallies From Halftime Deficit, Pulls Away From UNLV 36-24

UNLV carried a 24-23 lead into the break after Kasey Bell’s field goal as time expired in the second quarter. Wyoming answered midway through the third when **Damashja Harris punched in a rushing touchdown at 5:33 to give the Cowboys a 29-24 lead**, though the two-point attempt failed.

That score marked the final lead change in a game that featured seven. From there, Wyoming’s defense held UNLV scoreless over the final two quarters.

The Cowboys added the decisive margin late in the fourth when **Sam Scott’s 1-yard touchdown run with 1:56 remaining pushed the lead to 36-24**, sealing the outcome.

## Anderson leads high-powered passing attack

Quarterback Kaden Anderson continued his strong run of play, finishing **25 of 36 for 386 yards and three touchdowns** with no turnovers. He distributed the ball across multiple targets in a passing attack that produced 386 yards.

Running back Terron Kellman delivered a dual-threat performance, rushing **20 times for 114 yards** and adding **seven receptions for 130 yards and a touchdown**. His touchdown catch at the end of the first quarter gave Wyoming its first lead at 9-7.

Wide receiver Michael Fitzgerald added **seven catches for 97 yards and a touchdown**, while Jaylen Sargent contributed **six receptions for 50 yards and a score**, including a go-ahead touchdown late in the second quarter that briefly put Wyoming in front 23-21.

After halftime, Wyoming controlled the scoring. Harris’ third-quarter touchdown provided the lead for good, and the Cowboys added Scott’s late fourth-quarter score to create separation.

UNLV’s offense, which totaled 513 yards, did not score after halftime despite a strong performance from Colandrea, who finished **23 of 28 for 361 yards and three touchdowns**. Omeire led the Rebels with **six catches for 80 yards and two touchdowns**, while Keyvone Lee added 63 receiving yards.

Wyoming countered with 581 total yards and 28 first downs, finishing without a turnover.

## Closing outlook

Wyoming’s undefeated start now stands at 5-0, with the Cowboys continuing to stack wins through a combination of high-volume passing and timely scoring. The ability to erase a halftime deficit and shut out UNLV in the second half adds another dimension to an already productive start.

For UNLV, a 24-point first half was undone by a scoreless second half, leaving the Rebels at 3-2.

# Wyoming Pulls Away Late, Stays Unbeaten with 42–19 Win at Air Force

Colorado Springs, CO — Wyoming broke open a tight game with a three-touchdown burst in the fourth quarter, turning a one-score margin into a **42–19 victory** over Air Force on Saturday.

The Cowboys led just 21–13 entering the final period before scoring three touchdowns in a span of just over three minutes of game time, creating separation in a matchup that was close for much of the afternoon. Wyoming improves to 7–0 with the win, extending its winning streak to seven games.

## Fourth-quarter surge breaks it open

Air Force trimmed the deficit to eight points on a 6:02 field goal from Reagan Tubbs, making it 21–13 early in the fourth quarter. Wyoming answered almost immediately.

**Terron Kellman scored on a rushing touchdown just 14 seconds later**, pushing the lead to 28–13. On the Cowboys’ next possession, **Kaden Anderson connected with John Michael Gyllenborg for a touchdown with 4:48 remaining**, extending the advantage to 35–13.

Air Force added a rushing touchdown from Tylor Latham with 2:50 left but failed on the two-point attempt. Wyoming responded again, as **Anderson found Michael Fitzgerald for a touchdown with 2:29 remaining**, capping the scoring at 42–19.

## Anderson’s efficiency fuels offense

Anderson delivered one of his most efficient performances of the season, completing **20 of 24 passes for 199 yards and four touchdowns** without a turnover. He spread the ball across multiple targets while maintaining steady production throughout the game.

Gyllenborg was a key red-zone presence, finishing with **three receptions for 40 yards and two touchdowns**. Fitzgerald added **six catches for 48 yards and a touchdown**, while Kellman contributed both as a runner and receiver with **10 carries for 138 yards and a touchdown** along with six receptions for 59 yards.

Damashja Harris added a short rushing touchdown in the third quarter as Wyoming built its lead.

## Early deficit, steady control

Air Force struck first when Liam Szarka scored on a rushing touchdown with 4:39 remaining in the first quarter for a 7–0 lead. Wyoming answered late in the quarter as **Anderson hit Jaylen Sargent for a touchdown with 44 seconds left**, tying the game at 7–7.

Wyoming took the lead in the second quarter when **Anderson connected with Gyllenborg at the 5:03 mark**, making it 14–7. Air Force closed the half with a field goal from Tubbs with nine seconds remaining, but Wyoming carried a 14–10 advantage into halftime.

The Cowboys extended the margin in the third quarter on **Harris’ rushing touchdown with 2:50 remaining**, pushing the lead to 21–10 and setting up the decisive fourth quarter.

## Defensive effort holds firm

Wyoming’s defense allowed 428 total yards but limited scoring opportunities, holding Air Force to 19 points and no passing touchdowns. The Falcons were held to 5 of 14 on third down.

Andrew Johnson led the Cowboys with **10 tackles**, while Dainsus Miller added nine and Desman Hearns contributed six.

Szarka paced Air Force with **17 completions on 27 attempts for 213 yards** while also rushing for 109 yards and a touchdown on 17 carries. The Falcons totaled 169 rushing yards on 35 attempts.

## Big picture

Wyoming’s unbeaten start continues at 7–0, with another performance defined by efficient quarterback play and timely scoring runs. The Cowboys have now scored at least 36 points in each of their last three games.

Air Force falls to 3–1, unable to match Wyoming’s scoring output in the final quarter after keeping the game within one possession through three periods.

Wyoming heads into the next week still undefeated, with its offense and late-game execution continuing to define its season.

# No. 17 Wyoming Surges Past Boise State with Second-Half Explosion

Boise, ID — Wyoming turned a three-point halftime deficit into a runaway result, scoring 35 points after the break to defeat Boise State **45–27** and remain unbeaten at 9–0.

The No. 17 Cowboys trailed 13–10 at halftime before overwhelming Boise State with a dominant third quarter and a decisive stretch early in the fourth. Wyoming, which entered at 8–0, extended its winning streak with a performance that flipped quickly after the break.

## Third-Quarter Swing Breaks It Open

Wyoming seized control in a frantic third quarter that featured four touchdowns in under four minutes of game time.

After Boise State carried a 13–10 lead into halftime, the Cowboys struck first in the second half. **Kaden Anderson connected with Michael Fitzgerald for a touchdown at 4:30**, giving Wyoming its first lead at 17–13.

Boise State answered immediately, as **Greg Ard found Dylan Riley for a touchdown just 50 seconds later**, pushing the Broncos back in front, 20–17.

That would be the last time Wyoming trailed.

**Terron Kellman scored on a rushing touchdown at 2:22 to reclaim the lead**, and less than two minutes later, he added another rushing score at 0:48. In a span of under two minutes, Wyoming turned a three-point deficit into a **31–20 advantage** heading into the fourth.

## Anderson Efficient, Kellman Explosive

Wyoming’s offense balanced efficiency and explosiveness behind Anderson and Kellman.

Anderson, a redshirt sophomore quarterback, completed **20 of 26 passes for 235 yards and three touchdowns**, continuing a strong stretch of play. He spread the ball effectively, with three different receivers catching at least five passes.

Kellman delivered the game’s most dynamic performance. The redshirt junior running back carried the ball **nine times for 168 yards and two touchdowns**, averaging over 18 yards per carry, and added **five receptions for 54 yards and a receiving touchdown**. His two quick rushing scores in the third quarter created the separation Wyoming never relinquished.

John Michael Gyllenborg led the receiving corps with **five catches for 87 yards**, while Jaylen Sargent added **five receptions for 63 yards**.

## Defense Closes the Door

Wyoming’s defense bent but delivered the game’s defining play in the fourth quarter.

After Kellman’s receiving touchdown from Anderson extended the lead to 38–20 with 3:28 remaining, **cornerback Ian Bell intercepted Maddux Madsen and returned it for a touchdown at 2:12**, pushing the margin to **45–20** and effectively ending the game.

Bell also recorded nine tackles, matching his impact in coverage with production against the run. Linebacker Ethan Stuhlsatz led the team with **13 tackles**, while Andrew Johnson added nine.

Boise State moved the ball consistently, finishing with **575 total yards** and 335 passing yards from Madsen and Ard combined, but the Broncos’ lone turnover proved costly in a game that swung rapidly after halftime.

## Early Back-and-Forth

Boise State struck first on a **Maddux Madsen rushing touchdown with 3:13 left in the first quarter**, taking a 7–0 lead.

Wyoming answered in the second quarter when **Anderson found Fitzgerald for a touchdown at 8:57**, tying the game at 7–7. The teams traded field goals late in the half, with Erik Sandvik converting from 45 yards for Wyoming, but Boise State carried a **13–10 lead into halftime** after a Colton Boomer field goal as time expired.

That narrow edge vanished quickly in the third quarter as Wyoming’s offense accelerated.

## Finishing Kick

Boise State added a late touchdown with 10 seconds remaining on a **Maddux Madsen pass to Chris Marshall**, but the outcome had long been decided.

Wyoming finished with **506 total yards**, including 173 on the ground, and did not commit a turnover. Despite holding the ball for less time than Boise State, the Cowboys maximized their possessions with scoring efficiency.

The result keeps Wyoming perfect at 9–0 and adds another statement to a season defined by consistency and second-half control.

# Wyoming's Defense Turns Fresno State Into Another Victim as Cowboys Reach 10-0

Fresno, CA — Wyoming's season stopped being a novelty weeks ago. At 10-0 now, after a **20-7 road win over Fresno State**, the Cowboys are doing something sturdier than surprising people: they're dictating games on their terms, even when the offense doesn't explode.

That mattered Saturday night because Fresno State entered with the more talented roster profile, and Wyoming still spent most of the game making the Bulldogs look trapped in mud. Aaron Bohl's defense held Fresno State scoreless for more than three quarters, limited the Bulldogs to **2-for-10 on third down**, forced the game's only turnover and never allowed the hosts to possess the ball long enough to make the game uncomfortable until late in the fourth quarter.

The result pushed No. 14 Wyoming from 9-0 to **10-0**, strengthening the Cowboys' grip on the projected Group of Five automatic bid in the College Football Playoff field. Fresno State fell to 3-7.

## Wyoming controlled the game before the scoreboard reflected it

The final margin never felt enormous, but Wyoming owned the shape of the game almost from kickoff.

Brian Ostrander's offense didn't produce the explosive totals it had in recent weeks — the Cowboys entered averaging 38.5 points per game — yet Kaden Anderson stayed efficient and mistake-free. The redshirt sophomore quarterback completed **17 of 24 passes for 175 yards and a touchdown**, spreading the ball primarily to Michael Fitzgerald and tight end John Michael Gyllenborg while avoiding the turnover that could have opened the door for Fresno State.

Wyoming finally broke through midway through the second quarter when Anderson found Fitzgerald for the game's first touchdown with 7:06 remaining before halftime. Fitzgerald finished with **six catches for 58 yards and the score**, continuing a productive stretch after contributing in each of Wyoming's recent wins over Boise State and Colorado State.

The Cowboys added to the lead without their offense returning to the end zone. With 2:05 left in the half, Wyoming forced a safety to take a **9-0** advantage into the break.

That score fit the game. Fresno State moved the ball intermittently — E.J. Warner completed **23 of 28 passes for 204 yards and a touchdown** — but Wyoming consistently tightened in critical spots. The Bulldogs finished with just 52 rushing yards on 19 carries, and Warner lost yardage on the ground under steady pressure.

## Terron Kellman finished it

For most of the second half, Fresno State lingered within two possessions. Wyoming finally ended the suspense with 6:10 remaining.

Terron Kellman, who erupted for 168 rushing yards against Boise State a week earlier, delivered the decisive sequence Saturday. The redshirt junior running back punched in a rushing touchdown and Wyoming converted the ensuing two-point attempt to stretch the lead to **17-0**.

Kellman finished with **14 carries for 62 yards and a touchdown** while adding **five receptions for 46 yards**, giving Wyoming its most reliable all-purpose option on a night when every possession mattered.

Fresno State answered quickly. Warner connected with Josiah Freeman for a touchdown with 3:52 remaining, trimming the margin to 17-7 and briefly giving the Bulldogs life. Freeman led Fresno State receivers with **five catches for 68 yards and the score**.

Wyoming never let the game tighten further.

Anderson guided the Cowboys into range for Erik Sandvik, whose **33-yard field goal with 1:56 remaining** restored a two-score lead and effectively closed the night. Sandvik's kick capped Wyoming's final scoring drive and preserved a game script the Cowboys controlled from beginning to end.

## Defense keeps carrying Wyoming into bigger games

The offense generated only 247 total yards, and Wyoming's rushing attack managed 33 net yards after sacks and negative quarterback runs were factored in. None of it mattered because the Cowboys continue finding ways to make opponents play left-handed.

Andrew Johnson led Wyoming with **eight tackles**, Ian Bell added seven and Ethan Stuhlsatz contributed five more as the Cowboys limited Fresno State to seven points despite allowing 259 total yards.

That's the part of Wyoming's unbeaten run becoming hardest to dismiss. The Cowboys have won shootouts this season — including the 45-27 victory over Boise State last week — but Saturday showed another version of the team. One that can win ugly, shorten games and trust its defense to finish the work.

At 10-0, Wyoming no longer looks like a fun September story. The Cowboys look built for November.

# No. 14 Wyoming Turns Halftime Deficit Into Another Statement, Pulls Away From New Mexico 35-14

Laramie, WY — Wyoming spent 30 minutes looking vulnerable for the first time in weeks. Then the Cowboys looked like a team headed toward December football again.

**No. 14 Wyoming erased a halftime deficit and shut out New Mexico over the final two quarters Saturday, scoring 22 unanswered points in a 35-14 win that pushed the Cowboys to 11-0.** A game that sat on edge at 14-13 late in the second quarter flipped completely after Wyoming's defense forced three interceptions from New Mexico quarterback Jack Layne and the offense found rhythm through quarterback Kaden Anderson and running back Terron Kellman.

The Cowboys entered the week projected as the Group of Five automatic qualifier in the 12-team College Football Playoff field, and for a half, New Mexico looked capable of disrupting that conversation. Instead, Wyoming extended its winning streak to 11 games and stayed unbeaten heading into the final stretch of Mountain West play.

## Wyoming's third quarter changed everything

New Mexico carried a 14-13 lead into halftime after DJ McKinney's rushing touchdown with 28 seconds left in the second quarter. Wyoming answered immediately before the break with **Erik Sandvik's second field goal of the half as time expired**, trimming the deficit to one.

That momentum carried into the third quarter.

At the 4:25 mark, Anderson found senior wide receiver Michael Fitzgerald for the go-ahead touchdown, and Wyoming added the two-point conversion to stretch the lead to 21-14. It was the game's decisive swing. New Mexico never scored again.

Aaron Bohl's defense took over from there. Layne, who threw for 191 yards and a touchdown, also tossed three interceptions as Wyoming tightened its coverage and consistently forced the Lobos into difficult passing situations. The Cowboys limited New Mexico to 75 rushing yards and just 12 first downs.

The knockout punch came midway through the fourth quarter when **sophomore safety Jones Thomas intercepted Layne and returned it for a touchdown**, extending Wyoming's lead to 28-14 with 8:44 remaining.

Less than two minutes later, Kellman broke through for his own touchdown run to make it 35-14 and complete the turnaround from Wyoming's largest deficit of the day.

## Anderson efficient again, Kellman keeps rolling

Wyoming's offense did not overwhelm New Mexico with explosive volume, but it consistently controlled possessions and avoided negative plays after halftime.

**Anderson completed 25 of 31 passes for 243 yards, one touchdown and one interception while adding a rushing score.** The redshirt sophomore quarterback completed better than 80 percent of his throws and distributed the ball across multiple targets.

Fitzgerald led the receiving corps with **nine catches for 87 yards and a touchdown**, continuing a productive stretch that has become increasingly important in Wyoming's passing game. Tight end John Michael Gyllenborg added six receptions for 61 yards, while Jaylen Sargent caught four passes for 33 yards.

Kellman delivered the most explosive performance of the afternoon. The redshirt junior running back carried 14 times for **148 yards and a touchdown**, another major outing after his 168-yard game against Boise State two weeks earlier.

Wyoming finished with 488 total yards and averaged more than seven yards per offensive play between the passing and rushing attacks combined.

## New Mexico struck first and challenged Wyoming early

The Lobos arrived at 3-7 and on a three-game losing streak, but they controlled much of the first half.

Layne opened the scoring with a touchdown pass to Damon Bankston at the 5:22 mark of the first quarter, giving New Mexico a 7-0 lead. Wyoming answered on Sandvik's 47-yard field goal late in the quarter, then moved ahead early in the second when Anderson scored on the ground to make it 10-7.

New Mexico reclaimed the lead in the final minute of the half behind McKinney's touchdown run, briefly putting pressure on a Wyoming team that had spent much of the season dictating games early.

Instead, the Cowboys responded with their cleanest football after halftime.

Brian Ostrander's offense committed just one turnover, converted four of eight third downs and steadily wore down the Lobos over the final two quarters. Wyoming outgained New Mexico 488-266 overall and finished plus-two in turnover margin.

The final score looked comfortable. For a half, it wasn't. But Wyoming's second-half control looked familiar — the offense efficient, the defense opportunistic, and another unbeaten week secured.

# Wyoming’s Perfect Season Survived a Third-Quarter Collapse

Laramie, WY — Wyoming spent three quarters flirting with the kind of loss that reshapes an unbeaten season, then trusted the same formula that carried it to 11 straight wins: a timely throw from Kaden Anderson and a fourth-quarter run game that refused to disappear.

The No. 13 Cowboys erased a seven-point deficit in the final seven minutes Saturday night, beating Hawaii 35-28 to finish the regular season 12-0. Wyoming entered the fourth quarter trailing after surrendering 15 unanswered points in the third, but Anderson’s touchdown pass to Michael Fitzgerald and Terron Kellman’s late scoring run rescued a game that had tilted hard toward the Rainbow Warriors.

Barely.

For a team that had won by an average margin of more than two touchdowns entering the week, this looked different. Hawaii, which arrived at 5-6 and already eliminated from bowl contention, pushed Wyoming into its tightest corner in weeks behind quarterback Micah Alejado’s **29-for-38, 475-yard, three-touchdown** night.

Still, Wyoming left undefeated.

## The seven-minute stretch that saved the Cowboys

Hawaii carried a 28-21 lead into the fourth after scoring twice in the final 4:36 of the third quarter. Alejado first found Landon Sims for a touchdown with 4:36 remaining in the quarter, then connected with Jackson Harris for another scoring strike with 51 seconds left to give the Rainbow Warriors their first lead of the night.

Wyoming’s response arrived late but arrived all the same.

Anderson directed the tying drive midway through the fourth quarter and hit Fitzgerald for a touchdown with 6:34 remaining, evening the score at 28-28. Fitzgerald, a senior wide receiver who has become one of Wyoming’s most reliable targets down the stretch of the season, finished with **six catches for 72 yards and a touchdown**.

Then came Kellman.

The redshirt junior running back capped Wyoming’s final scoring drive with a touchdown run at the 1:20 mark, pushing the Cowboys back in front 35-28 after they had trailed by seven earlier in the half. Kellman carried 12 times for 79 yards, continuing a productive late-season stretch that included a 148-yard game against New Mexico a week earlier.

Wyoming’s defense bent throughout the night against Hawaii’s passing attack. It did not force many empty possessions. But after the Kellman score, the Cowboys protected the lead they had reclaimed.

That was enough.

## Anderson steadied Wyoming after the game turned

Anderson never produced a gaudy stat line, but Wyoming needed efficiency more than fireworks once Hawaii seized control in the third quarter. The redshirt sophomore quarterback completed **20 of 28 passes for 236 yards, two touchdowns and one interception**, adding nine rushing yards on the ground.

His first touchdown pass came early. With 7:07 left in the opening quarter, Anderson connected with Jackson Holman to give Wyoming a 7-0 lead. Later, after Hawaii had trimmed the margin to 14-10 in the second quarter, Anderson helped set up Sam Scott’s second rushing touchdown with 1:36 remaining before halftime.

Wyoming led 21-13 at the break despite Hawaii already showing signs that its passing game could stress the Cowboys vertically. Alejado threw for 475 yards, and Harris alone accounted for **nine catches and 148 yards**.

There is probably a larger question here about Wyoming’s secondary entering postseason play. A defense that had allowed 20.8 points per game entering Saturday surrendered 475 passing yards to a team that entered the night under .500. One game does not redefine a season, but it does sharpen the conversation.

The offense answered anyway.

## Sam Scott’s first half kept Wyoming in control

Before Hawaii’s third-quarter surge, Wyoming looked ready to cruise behind Scott and a running game that consistently created manageable situations.

Scott scored on a rushing touchdown with 15 seconds left in the first quarter to make it 14-3, then added another with 1:36 remaining in the second. The redshirt senior finished with **13 carries for 88 yards and two touchdowns**, while also catching three passes for 20 yards.

Wyoming’s balance mattered. The Cowboys finished with **507 total yards**, averaged nearly 6.8 yards per play based on their listed offensive totals, and converted four of seven third downs. They also controlled possession despite Hawaii’s explosive passing numbers.

Hawaii never established anything on the ground. Officially, the Rainbow Warriors finished with **minus-36 rushing yards on nine carries**, largely because Wyoming consistently disrupted Alejado and Sims behind the line. The Cowboys did not dominate the box score defensively overall, but they forced Hawaii into a one-dimensional game.

That almost was not enough.

## A perfect record, and a warning attached to it

Wyoming entered November ranked No. 17. It leaves the regular season unbeaten and still climbing after victories over Boise State, Fresno State and now Hawaii to close the year.

Saturday also delivered a reminder that the margin for an undefeated team can disappear quickly.

The Cowboys scored first, led at halftime and still found themselves chasing the game late in the third quarter after Hawaii ripped through the secondary for two touchdowns in less than four minutes. Against most Mountain West opponents this season, Wyoming’s balance on offense and turnover margin had insulated it from stretches like that. Against Hawaii, the Cowboys needed one more fourth-quarter drive to keep the season untouched.

And they got it.

# Wyoming shuts out Boise State early, wins Mountain West title 44-14

Wyoming claimed the Mountain West championship with a 44-14 victory over Boise State on Saturday in a neutral-site game. The Cowboys finished the season 12-1 after entering the game undefeated and projected as the Group of Five automatic qualifier in the College Football Playoff field.

Redshirt sophomore quarterback Kaden Anderson completed **20 of 23 passes for 241 yards and three touchdowns** and added a rushing score for Wyoming. The Cowboys finished with **462 total yards**, rushed for 182 yards on 18 carries and did not commit a turnover against a Boise State team that entered the game with a three-game winning streak.

Wyoming, which had already beaten Boise State 45-27 in Week 11, built a 21-0 halftime lead and never trailed. Anderson connected with redshirt senior tight end John Michael Gyllenborg for a touchdown in the opening quarter, then found Sam Scott for another score in the second quarter before senior linebacker Ethan Stuhlsatz returned a fumble for a touchdown with 1:16 left before halftime.

The Cowboys pushed the margin to 30-0 midway through the third quarter. Anderson hit senior wide receiver Michael Fitzgerald for a touchdown with 5:24 remaining in the quarter, and Erik Sandvik added a 28-yard field goal less than a minute later.

Fitzgerald finished with **eight catches for 98 yards and a touchdown**, while Gyllenborg added four receptions for 63 yards and a score. Scott contributed 96 rushing yards on eight carries and caught three passes for 43 yards and a touchdown. Terron Kellman added 66 rushing yards and scored on a fourth-quarter touchdown run.

Boise State avoided the shutout when Maddux Madsen threw a touchdown pass to Chris Marshall late in the third quarter. Marshall caught another touchdown pass in the fourth, but Wyoming answered with scoring runs from Kellman and Anderson to maintain control.

Madsen completed 29 of 40 passes for 266 yards and two touchdowns for Boise State, which finished 9-4. Latrell Caples caught eight passes for 125 yards, while Marshall accounted for both Broncos touchdowns through the air.

Wyoming's defense limited Boise State to 57 rushing yards and forced two turnovers. Brayden Johnson and Andrew Johnson each recorded eight tackles, while Stuhlsatz added seven tackles to go with his fumble return touchdown.

The Cowboys closed the year with a 12-1 record after extending a season that included wins over Utah, Colorado and Boise State twice. Boise State finished 9-4 after winning four of its final five regular-season games before the championship matchup.

# No. 7 Wyoming falls to Oklahoma 28-21 in CFP first round

No. 7 Wyoming saw its unbeaten season end Saturday with a 28-21 loss to No. 10 Oklahoma in a College Football Playoff first-round game.

The Cowboys finished 13-1 after entering the postseason on a 13-game winning streak, while Oklahoma advanced to the quarterfinals against Miami in the Cotton Bowl.

Wyoming quarterback Kaden Anderson completed **36 of 50 passes for 333 yards and two touchdowns**, but Oklahoma built a three-score first-half lead and never trailed. Tight end John Michael Gyllenborg added **nine catches for 127 yards**, and wide receiver Michael Fitzgerald caught eight passes for 89 yards.

The Cowboys outgained Oklahoma **485-348** and held the ball for more than 22 minutes, but Oklahoma converted its limited possessions into points behind quarterback John Mateer and running back Jaydn Ott. Mateer completed 19 of 25 passes for 216 yards and two touchdowns while adding a rushing score. Ott ran 10 times for 113 yards and a touchdown and caught a touchdown pass.

Oklahoma opened the scoring on Mateer's rushing touchdown midway through the first quarter before Wyoming answered with a 47-yard field goal from Erik Sandvik. The Sooners pushed the lead back to 14-3 when Ott scored on a rushing touchdown with 16 seconds left in the opening quarter.

The Sooners stretched the margin to 21-3 in the second quarter on Mateer's touchdown pass to Isaiah Sategna, who finished with nine catches for 133 yards. Wyoming answered before halftime when Anderson connected with Terron Kellman for a touchdown to cut the deficit to 21-10.

Wyoming's comeback bid took shape in the third quarter. Anderson found Eric Richardson for a touchdown with 4:31 left in the quarter, and the Cowboys converted the 2-point attempt to pull within 21-18 after overcoming an 18-point deficit.

The Cowboys' defense held Oklahoma scoreless through the third quarter, but the Sooners answered in the fourth when Mateer connected with Ott on a touchdown pass for a 28-18 lead with 7:08 remaining.

Sandvik added his second field goal with 1:21 left to bring Wyoming within one score again, but the Cowboys could not complete the final drive they needed.

Brian Ostrander's offense moved the ball consistently throughout the game, finishing with 20 first downs and 333 passing yards. Kellman added 62 rushing yards on five carries while also catching seven passes and a touchdown.

The loss ended Wyoming's breakthrough CFP run after the Cowboys climbed from No. 17 in Week 11 to a top-10 ranking entering the postseason. Oklahoma improved to 11-2 and moved on to face No. 2 seed Miami in the quarterfinal round.

# Hellums, Deblanc lead Wyoming past Charlotte 55-35

Laramie, WY — Senior quarterback Cale Hellums threw for four touchdowns and Wyoming opened the 2026 season with a 55-35 victory over Charlotte on Saturday. The Cowboys, coming off a 13-1 season and a No. 11 finish in the final 2025 poll, moved to 1-0 after never trailing.

Hellums completed **25 of 35 passes for 380 yards and four touchdowns**, all to redshirt freshman wide receiver Deion Deblanc. Deblanc finished with **13 catches for 173 yards and four scores**, while redshirt senior running back Terron Kellman added 241 rushing yards and two touchdowns on 20 carries as Wyoming piled up 705 total yards.

Charlotte quarterback Grayson Loftis kept the 49ers within reach through the first half, throwing for 382 yards and four touchdowns. Jariel Cobb accounted for five touchdowns, catching six passes for 205 yards and four scores while adding 114 rushing yards and another touchdown on the ground, but Charlotte dropped to 0-1 after allowing 55 points.

Wyoming struck first on a touchdown pass from Hellums to Deblanc midway through the opening quarter before Charlotte answered twice through the Loftis-to-Cobb connection. Kellman’s rushing touchdown with 1:45 left in the first quarter restored the Cowboys’ lead at 14-7, and Wyoming never gave it back after Charlotte tied the game again less than a minute later.

The Cowboys created separation in the second quarter. Hellums connected with Deblanc for another touchdown, Erik Sandvik added a field goal, and Kellman scored his second rushing touchdown with 14 seconds left in the half. Charlotte answered with Cobb’s rushing touchdown four seconds before halftime, trimming Wyoming’s lead to 31-28 at the break.

Wyoming’s offense continued producing after halftime while the defense limited Charlotte to one second-half touchdown. Hellums found Deblanc again midway through the third quarter to extend the lead to 38-28, and the same combination connected for a fourth touchdown with 6:58 remaining in the game.

Charlotte pulled within 10 after Loftis hit Cobb for their fourth touchdown connection of the night, but Wyoming answered again. Nico Hamilton scored on a 1-yard run with 2:58 left, and Sandvik’s second field goal pushed the margin to the final 20-point difference.

The Cowboys finished with 30 first downs and averaged more than 28 yards per scoring drive. Tight end Landon Pace added eight catches for 159 yards in the victory, while Sandvik converted both of his field-goal attempts, including a long of 43 yards.

Wyoming opened the season 1-0 after last year’s bowl loss, while Charlotte began the year 0-1 despite 541 total yards and five touchdowns from Cobb.

# No. 25 Wyoming upsets No. 22 Kansas State 31-24 in overtime

Laramie, WY — No. 25 Wyoming beat No. 22 Kansas State 31-24 in overtime on Saturday, giving the Cowboys an early-season ranked win after last year's 13-1 finish and final No. 11 ranking. Wyoming improved to 2-0 after overcoming a seven-point deficit in the second quarter.

Senior quarterback **Cale Hellums completed 25 of 36 passes for 314 yards and a touchdown while adding 39 rushing yards and two rushing scores**. Wyoming finished with **515 total yards and 26 first downs**, controlling possession despite committing three turnovers.

Kansas State quarterback **Blake Barnett threw for 255 yards and three touchdowns** as the Wildcats erased an early 10-point deficit and later forced overtime with a late fourth-quarter scoring drive. Garrett Oakley caught four passes for 77 yards and a touchdown, while Sutton Smith added a 68-yard receiving day with a score.

Wyoming struck first with Erik Sandvik's 39-yard field goal before Hellums added a rushing touchdown later in the opening quarter for a 10-0 lead. Kansas State answered with Eric Robledo's field goal as time expired in the quarter, then tied the game in the second on Barnett's touchdown pass to Sutton Smith.

Barnett gave the Wildcats their first lead with 2:06 left in the first half when he connected with Oakley for a touchdown and a 17-10 advantage. Wyoming answered before halftime as Hellums found redshirt freshman wide receiver **Deion Deblanc for a touchdown with 44 seconds remaining**, sending the teams into the break tied 17-17.

The teams remained tied entering the fourth quarter before Hellums scored his second rushing touchdown with 1:25 remaining to give Wyoming a 24-17 lead. Kansas State answered quickly when Barnett found Jamal Roberts for the tying touchdown with 35 seconds left in regulation.

Wyoming ended the game in overtime on Nico Hamilton's 1-yard touchdown run after Kansas State failed to score on its possession. Hamilton finished with one carry, but it delivered the decisive points.

Deblanc led Wyoming receivers with **10 catches for 119 yards and a touchdown**, while running back Terron Kellman added 87 rushing yards and 65 receiving yards. Wyoming offensive coordinator Brian Ostrander's offense averaged more than 14 yards per completion against a Kansas State defense that had allowed seven points in its opener.

Kansas State dropped to 1-1 after opening the season with a 49-7 win over FCS East Judicials. Wyoming's victory gave the Mountain West program a ranked nonconference win at home as the Cowboys moved to 2-0.

# No. 19 Wyoming routs Arkansas 44-6 behind Hellums, defense

Fayetteville, AR — No. 19 Wyoming beat Arkansas 44-6 on Saturday, improving to 3-0 after a dominant road performance against an SEC opponent. The Cowboys, who finished 13-1 and ranked No. 11 in the final poll last season, have now opened 2026 with wins over Kansas State and Arkansas.

Senior quarterback Cale Hellums accounted for five touchdowns as Wyoming never trailed. Hellums completed **23 of 38 passes for 263 yards and two touchdowns** while adding **three rushing scores** on the ground. Redshirt senior running back Terron Kellman added 128 rushing yards on 14 carries, and redshirt sophomore wide receiver Tyler Nystrom caught nine passes for 108 yards and a touchdown.

Wyoming controlled the game from the opening quarter. Hellums scored on touchdown runs with 4:50 and 0:13 remaining in the first quarter, giving the Cowboys a 14-0 lead after one period. Erik Sandvik extended the margin with field goals in the second quarter, including a **54-yard kick**, before Hellums found sophomore tight end Landon Pace for a touchdown pass with 20 seconds left in the half.

The Cowboys led 27-3 at halftime and continued to separate in the third quarter. Hellums added another rushing touchdown midway through the period, then connected with Nystrom for a touchdown pass with 53 seconds left in the quarter to push the lead to 41-3.

Wyoming finished with **497 total yards and 22 first downs**, compared to seven first downs for Arkansas. The Cowboys also forced three turnovers while committing none. Aaron Bohl's defense limited Arkansas to 19 rushing yards on 10 attempts and intercepted quarterback KJ Jackson three times.

Jackson completed 10 of 25 passes for 126 yards before Drake Lindsey entered and went 11 of 13 for 87 yards. Jalen Brown led Arkansas receivers with four catches for 92 yards. Defensively, Ahkhari Johnson recorded nine tackles for the Razorbacks.

Sandvik closed the scoring with his third field goal of the game late in the fourth quarter. Arkansas' only points came on two Scott Starzyk field goals.

The loss dropped Arkansas to 2-1 after opening the season with wins over North Carolina State and Indiana. Wyoming entered the game projected as the Group of Five automatic qualifier in the College Football Playoff field and added another road win to a résumé that already included a victory over then-No. 22 Kansas State.

# No. 18 Wyoming beats Fresno State 56-27 to stay unbeaten

Laramie, WY — No. 18 Wyoming defeated Fresno State 56-27 on Saturday in a matchup of unbeaten Mountain West teams, improving to 4-0 on the season. The Cowboys, who entered the game riding a three-game winning streak after finishing 2025 ranked No. 11 and reaching a bowl game, remained unbeaten behind a prolific passing performance and a decisive fourth-quarter surge.

Senior quarterback **Cale Hellums completed 18 of 33 passes for 415 yards and six touchdowns**, leading an offense that generated **613 total yards on only 45 plays**. Redshirt freshman wide receiver **Deion Deblanc caught five passes for 109 yards and three touchdowns**, while redshirt senior running back Terron Kellman added 118 receiving yards and a touchdown on five catches and rushed for 66 yards and another score. Wyoming never turned the ball over and finished with six touchdown passes from Hellums, including scoring strikes to Tyler Nystrom, Deblanc, Kellman and Landon Pace.

Fresno State stayed within striking distance for much of the afternoon. After Wyoming opened the scoring on a Hellums-to-Nystrom touchdown, the Bulldogs answered with a field goal and later took a 17-16 lead in the second quarter on touchdown passes from **Aidan Armenta** to Ezekiel Avit and Devon Rivers. Wyoming responded immediately when Hellums found Kellman for a touchdown with 1:32 remaining before halftime, sending the Cowboys into the break ahead 23-17. The Bulldogs produced significant offense in defeat, with **Armenta throwing for 412 yards and two touchdowns on 23-of-31 passing**, while Richie Anderson (105 yards) and Avit (95 yards, one touchdown) helped Fresno State finish with 419 total yards. But Wyoming's defense delivered the game's biggest turnover play in the fourth quarter when **Gary Rutherford returned an interception for a touchdown**, part of a stretch in which the Cowboys scored 27 points in the final period.

The victory marked Wyoming's second consecutive win over Fresno State after last season's 20-7 meeting and continued the Cowboys' strong start to 2026. Wyoming has now opened the year with victories over Charlotte, Kansas State, Arkansas and Fresno State, climbing from No. 25 to No. 18 in the rankings along the way. Fresno State entered the game at 3-0 after wins over Massachusetts, FCS West and UTSA, but fell to 3-1 despite outgaining Wyoming in first downs and receiving a 400-yard passing day from Armenta.

# 2025 Season Preview

## A Blank Slate

The 2025 season opens without a preseason Top 25 and without any prior-year results to shape expectations. There are no established rankings, no recent champions to defend a title, and no documented near-misses or breakthrough campaigns to carry forward. Every program across the FBS enters on equal footing, with the national picture still entirely undefined.

## What We Don’t Yet Know

With no historical data in place, there are no identifiable trends — no programs on sustained runs, no conference hierarchies, and no returning award winners to spotlight. Even the usual early signals of momentum or decline are absent. The landscape is, for now, completely open.

## The Season Ahead

That lack of structure makes the opening weeks especially important. The first results will effectively build the foundation for everything that follows — rankings, contenders, and storylines will all emerge in real time. Until then, the 2025 season begins as a true reset across the entire FBS.

# 2025 Week 1 Recap

## Headlines & Upsets

Week 1 opened with two tight ranked matchups, both decided by a field goal. In Miami, the **#4 Miami Hurricanes edged #16 Notre Dame 44-41**, outlasting one of the highest-scoring games of the weekend. Not far behind in intensity, **#2 Ohio State held off #11 Texas 23-21**, giving the Buckeyes an early statement win against a top-15 opponent.

Elsewhere, several ranked teams avoided danger, but not all escaped. The biggest upset came on the road, where **Cincinnati knocked off #22 Nebraska 38-35**, handing a ranked team an immediate loss to open the year. That result stands out in a week where most favorites took care of business.

Among ranked vs. ranked or Power-level matchups, **#10 Clemson controlled LSU 31-13**, delivering one of the more decisive wins between high-profile teams. Meanwhile, **#20 Alabama went on the road and beat Florida State 44-38**, surviving a competitive opener away from home.

## Around the Top 25

Beyond the headline games, most ranked teams handled their openers comfortably. **#1 Penn State rolled past Nevada 45-7**, while **#3 Georgia defeated Marshall 45-24**. **#12 Oregon dominated FCS Northwest 59-0**, and **#7 Oklahoma overwhelmed FCS Midwest 35-3**.

Several other programs posted high-scoring wins: **#13 Michigan beat New Mexico State 50-14**, **#9 Florida handled FCS East 38-10**, and **#17 Texas A&M topped UTSA 45-21**. **#23 USC** and **#14 Vanderbilt** both secured multi-score victories over FCS opponents, and **#24 Tennessee beat Syracuse 55-32** in one of the more offense-heavy games of the week.

Two additional ranked teams delivered lopsided results against FCS competition: **#21 Missouri won 63-16**, and **#18 Duke cruised 45-6**. Meanwhile, **#8 Indiana opened with a 41-14 win over Old Dominion**, rounding out a largely stable week for ranked programs outside of Nebraska’s loss.

## Around the Country

Outside the rankings, Week 1 produced a mix of shootouts, blowouts, and surprises. One of the highest-scoring games came in Akron, where **Wyoming outlasted Akron 45-38**. Similarly, **TCU edged North Carolina 32-30**, and **Boise State slipped past South Florida 27-24** in a trio of close finishes.

Several unranked teams delivered notable road wins. **East Carolina beat NC State 38-31**, while **Miami (OH) upset Wisconsin 37-34**. **Toledo’s 48-29 win over Kentucky** also stood out as a decisive result against a Power-level opponent.

A handful of games tilted heavily in one direction. **Baylor dominated Auburn 44-7**, and **Virginia shut out Coastal Carolina 41-0**. Meanwhile, **Georgia Tech defeated Colorado 17-13** in a lower-scoring contest, and **UTEP beat Utah State 38-10** on the road.

Elsewhere, several teams piled up points in comfortable wins, including **Houston (45-0)**, **Iowa State (48-0)**, **Louisville (52-0)**, and **Iowa (59-3)**, each opening the season with emphatic victories.

## Poll Movement

The first set of rankings following Week 1 reflects both stability at the top and movement behind it. **Penn State remains #1**, followed by **Georgia at #2** and **Oklahoma at #3**, with all three opening the season with wins.

Further down, **Clemson’s win over LSU lifts it to #4**, while **Oregon and Michigan slot in at #5 and #6** after decisive victories. Despite the loss, **LSU remains in the rankings at #8**, while **Indiana moves into the top 10 at #9**.

**Miami’s narrow win over Notre Dame positions it at #12**, while **Ohio State lands at #15 after its win over Texas**, with the Longhorns just behind at **#17**. Notably, **Nebraska remains ranked at #16 despite its loss**, indicating limited early fallout.

New names also appear deeper in the poll, including **Texas Tech (#20)** and **Navy (#22)**, both coming off strong opening wins. The bottom of the rankings features **Arizona State (#23)** and **BYU (#25)**, rounding out a Top 25 that begins to take shape after the first week of play.

# 2025 Week 2 Recap

## Headlines & Upsets

The most decisive statement of the week came in Norman, where **#3 Oklahoma overwhelmed #6 Michigan 41-15**, separating early in a top-10 matchup that never stayed competitive into the second half. It stands as the clearest gap between elite-ranked teams so far this season.

The upset board was active behind it. **Georgia Southern knocked off #23 USC 25-22**, handing the Trojans a narrow road loss in a game that tightened late. **Stanford also went on the road and beat #25 BYU 28-27**, taking a one-point result in one of the week’s closest finishes involving a ranked team.

Another ranked loss came out of the SEC matchup in Starkville, where **#23 Arizona State beat Mississippi State 44-21**, a comfortable road win that separates Arizona State early in its ranked profile.

The most surprising result outside the rankings came in Fayetteville, where **Arkansas State beat Arkansas 31-24**, delivering a straight-up in-state upset and one of the few Power-program losses to an unranked opponent this week.

## Around the Top 25

Most ranked teams handled business with varying levels of comfort. **#1 Penn State routed Florida International 69-10**, while **#2 Georgia defeated FCS Southeast 49-3**. **#5 Oregon beat Oklahoma State 53-21**, and **#4 Clemson handled Troy 49-14**, both in controlled, multi-score wins.

**#10 SMU edged Baylor 38-31**, one of the tighter ranked-team results against a Power opponent. **#11 Duke beat Illinois 34-24**, and **#12 Miami defeated FCS Southeast 27-3** to stay unbeaten. **#9 Indiana won 42-21 over Kennesaw State**, and **#8 LSU beat Louisiana Tech 44-10** in a comfortable outing.

Further down, **#19 Tennessee dominated FCS Southeast 59-3**, while **#17 Texas beat San Jose State 35-18**. **#15 Ohio State crushed FCS Southeast 56-0**, and **#16 Nebraska survived Akron 31-24**, one of the closer calls among ranked teams facing unranked opposition.

## Poll Movement

The post-Week 2 rankings reflect both stability at the top and notable movement behind it. **Penn State, Georgia, and Oklahoma remain the top three**, holding steady after dominant performances, including Oklahoma’s win over Michigan and Georgia’s rout.

**Oregon rises to #5**, and **Michigan settles at #6** following its loss. **Texas A&M enters at #7**, while **Notre Dame joins the poll at #13**, giving the top 15 a reshuffle driven by both results and early-season evaluations.

Further down, **Arizona State climbs to #23 after its win over Mississippi State**, while **BYU falls out of the top 25 after its one-point loss to Stanford**. **Tennessee moves up to #19**, and **Texas Tech lands at #20**, with both maintaining early momentum.

The bottom of the poll stabilizes around teams like **Missouri (#21)**, **Navy (#22)**, and **Brigham Young’s departure marking the only exit from last week’s group of ranked teams**.

## Around the Country

The unranked slate delivered a mix of blowouts and tight finishes. **Washington State’s 48-14 win over San Diego State** and **Colorado State’s 48-20 victory over FCS West** were among the more decisive results.

In tighter matchups, **Western Michigan edged North Texas 31-28**, and **Colorado survived Delaware 48-45** in one of the highest-scoring games of the week. **New Mexico State also pulled out a narrow 35-33 win over Tulsa**, adding another one-score result to a busy slate.

Elsewhere, several Power programs took surprising losses. **Wisconsin fell to Middle Tennessee State 34-31**, and **Arkansas State’s win over Arkansas 31-24** stood as the most notable upset involving a major-conference team.

Other unranked winners included **Jacksonville State over Liberty 42-21**, **East Carolina over FCS Southeast 49-13**, and **Appalachian State over FCS Midwest 52-27**, each contributing to a wide-ranging Week 2 across the FBS landscape.

# 2025 Week 3 Recap

## Headlines & Upsets

Week 3 delivered its clearest separation of contenders and challengers yet, starting with a statement in South Bend where **#7 Texas A&M crushed #13 Notre Dame 41-14**. The Aggies controlled the ranked matchup from start to finish, turning a top-15 road game into one of the most decisive results of the week.

The other ranked-vs-ranked showdown in Knoxville went the distance, where **#2 Georgia held off #19 Tennessee 38-33**. Tennessee kept pace throughout, but Georgia’s late edge preserved the Bulldogs’ unbeaten start in one of the week’s highest-scoring SEC clashes.

Upsets followed close behind. **Florida knocked off #8 LSU 28-21**, handing the Tigers a second loss and shaking up the upper tier of the rankings. In the Midwest, **Lafayette stunned #21 Missouri 29-28**, a one-score road upset that stands as one of the narrowest finishes of the week.

The volatility extended further down the rankings as **#23 Arizona State beat Mississippi State 44-21**, a comfortable road win that separated the Sun Devils from their SEC opponent. Meanwhile, USC’s 31-23 loss to Purdue and Kansas State’s 28-23 defeat to Arizona added to a broader week of ranked teams slipping against unranked opposition.

## Around the Top 25

Several top-ranked programs handled their assignments without much resistance. **#1 Penn State beat FCS East 38-7**, while **#5 Oregon defeated Northwestern 45-17**. **#6 Michigan rolled Central Michigan 54-18**, and **#4 Clemson defeated Georgia Tech 35-14**, all maintaining early-season momentum.
